theory.intro: The rays meeting at a single point divide a full turn into angles that together add to $360^{\circ}$. These are angles **at a point**.
facts: Angles **at a point** (around one vertex) add to $360^{\circ}$.
If all but one angle is known, subtract the total of the known angles from $360^{\circ}$.
steps: Check the angles all meet at one point and fill a full turn.
Subtract from $360^{\circ}$ to find the unknown.
